readings.title: JEDEC DDR5 SDRAM Standard (JESD79-5)
readings.note: The definitive specification for DDR5, including channel architecture (two independent 32-bit sub-channels), timing parameters (CL, tRCD, tRP, tRAS), on-die ECC, and speed grades from DDR5-4800 through DDR5-8800. The bandwidth calculations and timing analysis in Lecture 1 are derived directly from this standard.

readings.title: What is High Bandwidth Memory 3 (HBM3): Complete Engineering Guide
readings.note: Comprehensive technical overview of HBM3 architecture: TSV interconnects, 1024-bit interface, 16 independent channels, 3D stacking, and silicon interposer packaging. Includes bandwidth calculations showing how HBM3 achieves 819 GB/s per stack versus DDR5's 40-70 GB/s per channel.

readings.title: Coding for SSDs: Understanding Flash Technology
readings.note: Excellent multi-part series covering SSD internals: NAND flash physics, FTL mapping (page-level vs block-level), garbage collection, write amplification, wear leveling, and how application-level I/O patterns affect SSD performance. Essential background for Problem Set 4 Exercise 3 (FTL implementation).

readings.title: Introduction to Compute Express Link (CXL)
readings.authors: Donghyun Gouk et al., ACM Computing Surveys
readings.note: Peer-reviewed survey of CXL technology: sub-protocols (CXL.io, CXL.cache, CXL.mem), device types (Type 1/2/3), memory pooling, memory sharing, and cache coherency. Covers CXL 1.0 through 3.0 and analyzes latency characteristics of CXL-attached memory versus local DDR5.

readings.title: Digital Design and Computer Architecture: RISC-V Edition (Chapter 5: Digital Building Blocks)
readings.note: Chapter 5 covers memory arrays (SRAM, DRAM, ROM, flash), memory technologies, and error-correcting codes including Hamming codes. The treatment of SRAM 6T cell operation and DRAM read/write sequences aligns with this week's Lecture 1.
